name: Q743087
abstract: Alvin "Junior" Raglin (March 16, 1917 – November 10, 1955) was an American swing jazz double bassist. Raglin started out on guitar but had picked up bass by the mid-1930s.

works.t: Black, Brown and Beige: Three Dances

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: He had two bass players, Junior Raglin and – I can’t remember the name of the other bass player, but neither of them was there.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: Junior Raglin, whenever Duke would come to town would come by and sit and play. And I’d listen to these guys
about_quotes.doc_title: John Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Brown
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Oh yes, there was Junior Raglin... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, yes there was a lot of them, yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: Incidentally, that's the band Junior Raglin left here with Gene Cory in 1935. Sure enough.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ... [JUNIOR RAGLIN] There were a lot of good musicians there.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Now, well, Duke took Junior Raglin back for a while, you see, after Oscar.
